title: Young Love VII - The Lamp And The Star
content:
Yea, let me be 'thy bachelere,'
    'Tis sweeter than thy lord;
    How should I envy him, my dear,
    The lamp upon his board.
    Still make his little circle bright
    With boon of dear domestic light,
    While I afar,
    Watching his windows in the night,
    Worship a star
    For which he hath no bolt or bar.
    Yea, dear,
    Thy 'bachelere.'
meaning:
This evocative piece by Richard Le Gallienne, titled "Young Love VII - The Lamp And The Star", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
